description:
You are acting as a site-reliability engineer who has been paged to review a single-day uptime log for “site-01”.  
Everything you need is already on the filesystem; **do not download anything from the Internet**.

SYSTEM LAYOUT (already present before you start)
1. Log directory:  
   /home/user/logs  
2. Log file to inspect:  
   /home/user/logs/site-01-2023-10-05.log  

Your objectives (in order):

A. Create a new directory that will hold every artifact you produce.  
   • Path: /home/user/output  

B. Produce a *filtered* log that only contains the lines whose status word
   matches the regular-expression `(ERROR|CRITICAL|WARNING)` (case-sensitive).  
   • Output file: /home/user/output/site-01_alerts.log  
   • Keep the original line order exactly as it appears in the source file.  
   • The file must contain **no extra blank lines** and **no other log lines**.

C. Generate a plain-text incident report that summarizes what you found.  
   • Output file: /home/user/output/incident_report_2023-10-05.txt  
   • The report must follow **exactly** the 12-line template below
     (line-numbers are for clarity only and must NOT appear in the file):

     1: Incident Report: site-01  
     2: Date: 2023-10-05  
     3:  
     4: Total log entries: <TOTAL_COUNT>  
     5: OK entries: <OK_COUNT>  
     6: WARNING entries: <WARNING_COUNT>  
     7: ERROR entries: <ERROR_COUNT>  
     8: CRITICAL entries: <CRITICAL_COUNT>  
     9:  
    10: Alert log saved at: /home/user/output/site-01_alerts.log  
    11:  
    12: List of Alerts:  

     After the header’s 12th line, append one line per alert in the exact
     format shown here (the “#” column is a literal counter that must begin
     with 1 and increase sequentially):

     <N>. <TIMESTAMP> <STATUS> <MESSAGE>

     Do not indent any line; every line must end with a single newline
     character (UNIX line endings).

D. Verify your own work on the console:  
   1. Show the first five lines of /home/user/output/site-01_alerts.log  
   2. Show the entire /home/user/output/incident_report_2023-10-05.txt

DELIVERABLES THAT THE AUTOMATED TEST WILL CHECK
1. /home/user/output directory exists.  
2. /home/user/output/site-01_alerts.log exists and contains **exactly** the
   alert lines, in order, without additional data.  
3. /home/user/output/incident_report_2023-10-05.txt exists and matches the
   required template and counts.  
4. Console output demonstrating the verification steps in Objective D.

HINTS
• The raw log lines are space-delimited:  
  YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ site-01 STATUS [free-form message …]

• Typical tools: grep ‑E, awk, wc, sort, printf, etc.  
• You have normal user privileges and write access under /home/user.

When you are done, you should see only the designated files in /home/user/output and the incident report must present correct counts so the automated test can compare them byte-for-byte.
truth:
PRE-EXISTING FILES

1. Path: /home/user/logs/site-01-2023-10-05.log  
   Contents (exact, 7 lines, each terminated by \n):

2023-10-05T13:00:00Z site-01 OK response_time=120ms
2023-10-05T13:05:00Z site-01 ERROR connection_timeout
2023-10-05T13:10:00Z site-01 OK response_time=118ms
2023-10-05T13:15:00Z site-01 WARNING high_latency response_time=350ms
2023-10-05T13:20:00Z site-01 OK response_time=119ms
2023-10-05T13:25:00Z site-01 CRITICAL host_unreachable
2023-10-05T13:30:00Z site-01 OK response_time=117ms

EXPECTED FILES AFTER SUCCESSFUL TASK COMPLETION

A. /home/user/output/site-01_alerts.log  
   Contents (exact, 3 lines, each terminated by \n):

2023-10-05T13:05:00Z site-01 ERROR connection_timeout
2023-10-05T13:15:00Z site-01 WARNING high_latency response_time=350ms
2023-10-05T13:25:00Z site-01 CRITICAL host_unreachable

B. /home/user/output/incident_report_2023-10-05.txt  
   Contents (exact, 15 lines, each terminated by \n):

Incident Report: site-01
Date: 2023-10-05

Total log entries: 7
OK entries: 4
WARNING entries: 1
ERROR entries: 1
CRITICAL entries: 1

Alert log saved at: /home/user/output/site-01_alerts.log

List of Alerts:
1. 2023-10-05T13:05:00Z ERROR connection_timeout
2. 2023-10-05T13:15:00Z WARNING high_latency response_time=350ms
3. 2023-10-05T13:25:00Z CRITICAL host_unreachable

NOTE FOR TEST SUITE
• The test will fail if:
  – /home/user/output does not exist,
  – either file is missing or has extra / missing lines,
  – counts in the report do not match the ground truth above,
  – line endings differ from UNIX LF,
  – or verification output is absent from the console transcript.
